metadata.title: Display Sync Status Indicators (Green/Grey per Date)

story.iWant: to see which dates are synced with Google Calendar
story.soThat: I know whether the dates I am reviewing in the calendar are backed by synced Google data
story.tasks: - Verify Story 2.2 sync data and Story 3.1 calendar UI prerequisites before starting - Add SyncStatus enum plus ISyncStatusService and SyncStatusService - Compute per-date status from non-deleted gcal_event rows and read latest successful gcal refresh metadata - Refresh the visible-range status map after sync completion or manual Refresh Status - Render a shared green/grey indicator with last-sync tooltip in calendar views - Add service and view-model tests for status calculation and refresh behavior

acceptanceCriteria.ac: Each date cell shows green when at least one non-deleted gcal_event row exists for that date, and grey otherwise.
Indicators update immediately after sync completes without a manual page refresh.
Indicator tooltip shows "Last synced: X hours ago" using the latest successful gcal data_source_refresh timestamp.
Refresh Status recalculates indicator state from the current database contents.

doc.title: Epic 2 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Authoritative source for Story 2.4: status comes from non-deleted gcal_event presence by date, the tooltip comes from data_source_refresh metadata, and sync completion should trigger immediate UI refresh.

doc.title: Epic Breakdown
doc.snippet: Provides the business intent for green/grey indicators and the dependency note that calendar-view rendering relies on Epic 3 Story 3.1.

doc.title: UX Design Specification
doc.section: Tier 1 calendar indicators and top-strip status
doc.snippet: Confirms the Tier 1 experience expects green/grey indicators in calendar views and a visible last-sync affordance, but not on the settings page.

doc.title: Database Schemas
doc.snippet: Useful background for existing refresh metadata storage, but its older staleness-based sync-indicator language conflicts with the Epic 2 tech spec and should not override Story 2.4 implementation rules.

doc.title: Architecture
doc.snippet: Shows the intended calendar UI target surfaces and confirms Story 2.4 is a calendar-view feature, even though the live repo has not yet landed those UI files.

doc.title: Story 2.2
doc.snippet: Story 2.2 is responsible for writing gcal_event rows and data_source_refresh metadata. Story 2.4 must read that data rather than creating a second sync bookkeeping source.

file.reason: The current app still boots into SettingsPage. This is the concrete branch-shape dependency that blocks Story 2.4 UI work until Story 3.1 calendar views exist.
Already exposes GcalEvents and DataSourceRefreshes. Sync status should query these existing DbSets directly.
Provides StartDatetime, EndDatetime, IsAllDay, IsDeleted, and LastSyncedAt, which define per-date sync coverage.
Supplies LastRefreshedAt, SourceName, and Success used for last-sync tooltip text.
Confirms the current table and index names for refresh metadata reads; Story 2.4 should reuse them without schema changes.
Shows the repo already uses CommunityToolkit MVVM and WeakReferenceMessenger. Story 2.4 should reuse that messaging style for sync completion refresh, not invent a new pattern.
Current shell evidence only. Story 2.4 should not add indicator rendering here; it belongs in calendar views.
Current sync/auth service contract. Sync status refresh should listen to the completion path built around this sync infrastructure, not duplicate it.

component.name: successful data_source_refresh rows for source_name = gcal
manual sync completion notification or equivalent callback
visible date range ownership in a calendar view model

constraints.constraint: Use the live root-level Services/Data/ViewModels/Views layout. Do not introduce a new Core/Data project split for this story.
Do not implement sync indicator UI on SettingsPage. If Story 3.1 calendar views are absent on the branch, the UI portion of Story 2.4 is blocked until they land.
When docs conflict, follow docs/epic-2/tech-spec.md for Story 2.4. Do not use AppMetadata timestamp keys or stale-range logic from older planning docs as the primary status source.
Green/grey date status is derived from non-deleted gcal_event presence, while tooltip last-sync text is derived from the latest successful gcal data_source_refresh row.

Multi-day timed events must mark every intersected date. All-day events with midnight end boundaries must treat EndDatetime as exclusive so one-day all-day events do not bleed into the following date.
Do not add a dedicated sync-status table, cache table, or date_state usage in Story 2.4. Read from existing gcal_event and data_source_refresh only.
Reuse the existing CommunityToolkit.Mvvm messaging pattern and any sync-completion notification introduced by Story 2.2 / 2.5. Avoid duplicate message contracts.
Status loading should happen per visible range, not per cell, and should target sub-100 ms execution for about a month of dates using AsNoTracking and existing indexes.

tests.standards: xUnit + Moq + FluentAssertions. Prioritize unit tests for SyncStatusService and a view-model-level integration test for message-driven refresh. UI rendering automation is optional unless a calendar UI harness already exists on the branch.

ideas.test: Seed a non-deleted gcal_event for a date and verify the returned dictionary marks that date as Synced.
Seed only deleted rows for a date and verify the service still returns NotSynced.
Seed one timed event spanning multiple dates and verify every intersected date is marked Synced.
Seed an all-day event ending at midnight of the following day and verify only the intended day is marked Synced.
Seed successful and failed DataSourceRefresh rows across multiple sources and verify the latest successful gcal row is returned.
Simulate the sync-completion notification and verify the visible-range status map reloads without reconstructing the page.
